items.rationale: Sees blockchains enabling decentralized markets for ML compute that tap idle hardware, expanding available AI compute and countering big-tech compute dominance.

citations.quote: NEAR is emerging as the epicenter for AI x crypto.
Gensyn has the potential to 10-100x the available compute power for machine learning.

items.rationale: Treats faster, open-source proving as a core scalability unlock; champions Jolt and Lasso as step-change improvements in proving performance.

citations.quote: Today we're releasing Jolt Alpha. Fastest zkVM in the world. Best developer experience. 100% open source and a public good.
Lasso is already 10x faster than Halo2's lookup argument, even before optimizations.

items.rationale: Believes mainstream crypto adoption is impossible without privacy; excited by ZK approaches (Aleo, Aztec) that keep chains confidential yet fully verifiable, expanding the app design space.

citations.quote: Aleo's breakthrough is to use cutting edge cryptography in the form of zero knowledge proofs to create a blockchain that is privacy-preserving while still being fully verifiable.
It's hard to imagine crypto ever reaching mainstream adoption without privacy.

items.rationale: Views unbundling cryptoeconomic security from the EVM as a 100x innovation unlock — a 'verifiable cloud' letting developers extend Ethereum without spinning up new chains.

citations.quote: EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation on technologies like consensus mechanisms, novel virtual machines, decentralized oracles, bridges
EigenLayer is the verifiable cloud. Massive leap forward today toward the realization of that vision.

items.rationale: Backs the rollup-centric roadmap and sees shared sequencing (Espresso) as the fix for cross-rollup fragmentation; values L2s primarily for enabling permissionless innovation.

citations.quote: Espresso is building a decentralized network that acts as a coordination mechanism for rollup sequencers.
The most important benefit of L2s is not performance. It is that it unlocks permissionless innovation on top of Ethereum.

items.rationale: Convinced the future is multi-chain and that interoperability infra decoupling security from liveness (LayerZero v2) is critical plumbing.

citations.quote: LayerZero just launched v2. It radically improves on other approaches by cleanly decoupling security from liveness.
There is no longer any question that the future of crypto and web3 is multi-chain.

items.rationale: Wants to disintermediate the music industry, letting artists own contracts and connect directly with fans (Sound).
items.age_note: Core thesis from 2023-07-12, last touched 2023-09-25; ~12 months old and not reaffirmed in 2024.

citations.quote: Sound is music without the middleman; a web3 music platform that is powering the next generation of artists and their communities

items.rationale: Sees user-owned, permissionless messaging (XMTP) as core web3 infrastructure versus monolithic tech-giant platforms.
items.age_note: Thesis stated 2023-07-12; only a personal thank-you reference since (2024-02-22), not a fresh thesis restatement.

citations.quote: XMTP is emerging as a core messaging protocol for web3 — it is permissionless, open source, and is built on top of universal web3 protocols

items.rationale: His strongest concern — sees soulless memecoins, 100x-leverage perps, and parasitic airdrop farming as zero-sum degeneracy that actively holds the space back rather than bootstrapping it.

citations.quote: We don't need the casino. We never did. It's the primary force holding the space back.
Don't know who needs to hear it, but being a degen just isn't cool.

items.rationale: Worries the real existential risk is stagnation, and that bureaucrats deciding what can be built — plus a culture of safetyism — chokes permissionless innovation.

citations.quote: innovation dies when the decisions about what you CAN and CANNOT build are all made by web2 monopolies or by certain rogue, technically illiterate bureaucrats in Washington D.C.
We need to be taking more risks on new tech, not less.

items.rationale: Concerned that AI's massive compute requirements hand structural advantage to incumbents over startups, motivating decentralized compute as the counterweight.

citations.quote: building AI systems requires massive computational power, giving big tech companies an advantage over startups in the race to capture value from it.

items.rationale: Names cross-rollup composability breakdown as the biggest downside of the rollup-centric roadmap — though he frames it as a solvable problem (shared sequencing), not a reason to abandon the path.

citations.quote: The biggest disadvantage of a rollup-centric future is the fragmentation of the Ethereum ecosystem.

items.rationale: Surfaced loss-versus-rebalancing as a real, under-appreciated tradeoff for AMM LPs — a mild concern inferred from his endorsement of the explainer rather than an explicit warning.

citations.quote: I'd challenge anyone to provide a clearer explanation of the tradeoffs of participating as a liquidity provider in an AMM.

items.rationale: Singles out NEAR as the focal point of the AI-x-crypto thesis and increasingly aligned with the modular Ethereum stack.

citations.quote: NEAR is emerging as the epicenter for AI x crypto.

items.rationale: Excited by Aleo's live launch combining ZK confidentiality with verifiability, enabling private/compliant payments, identity, and hidden-information games.

citations.quote: Aleo network is live. Aleo introduces a new model for decentralized computation that combines confidentiality and scalability.

items.rationale: Led the Series B; sees restaking as a 'verifiable cloud' marketplace extending Ethereum's security to new decentralized services.

citations.quote: Today, we're announcing that we're leading EigenLayer's Series B.

items.rationale: Backs LayerZero as critical multi-chain infrastructure, praising the v2 security model that decouples security from liveness and makes execution permissionless.

citations.quote: LayerZero has built critical infrastructure that makes that possible.

items.rationale: Deeply Ethereum-aligned across his theses — restaking, rollups, shared sequencing, and privacy are all framed as extending Ethereum's pooled security and reach.

citations.quote: EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation ... in the same way that Ethereum unlocked 100x faster innovation at the application logic level

items.rationale: Rejects tokens that pump on memes with no purpose then collapse — emblematic of the 'casino' he sees as holding crypto back.

citations.quote: we don't need tokens without soul or purpose that moon on nothing but memes and then fall off a cliff.

risk_regime.rationale: A builder-bullish, structurally long-term posture: he explicitly urges taking MORE risk on new technology, framing stagnation as the true existential threat. But his risk appetite is bifurcated — strongly risk-on toward fundamental infrastructure (ZK, restaking, privacy, AI-compute, interoperability) and emphatically risk-off toward speculative/casino activity (memecoins, leverage, airdrop farming). Net: pro-innovation risk-on, not pro-speculation.

notes: Ali Yahya is an a16z crypto investor; nearly all statements are infrastructure/thesis-driven, not price or macro calls, so 'token' entries reflect protocol conviction inferred from his stated theses rather than trading views. Recency weighting: his most recent signals (2024-04 through 2024-09) reinforce — not reverse — earlier theses on ZK proving (Jolt), privacy (Aleo extends Aztec stance), restaking (EigenLayer), and AI-x-crypto (NEAR). No material reversals found in the corpus; NEAR appears consistently across 2023-11 and 2024-05. The anti-speculation/anti-'casino' stance (Dec 2023) is his most forceful concern and is consistent with his pro-fundamentals investing pattern, so treated as still-current despite not being restated verbatim in 2024. Web3 music (Sound) and messaging (XMTP) are marked persisted/aging — strong in 2023, only lightly touched in 2024. The LVR/AMM concern is extrapolated from an endorsement, not an explicit warning, hence low conviction. Throughout, his organizing frame is crypto as a tool for larger-scale human cooperation and permissionless innovation on Ethereum.
